Ad Text:
Company description:



Workplace Type: Hybrid


About Newmont


Newmont is the world's leading gold company and a producer of copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum. The company's world-class portfolio of assets, prospects and talent is anchored in favourable mining jurisdictions in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America and the Caribbean and North America.

Newmont is the only gold producer listed in the S&P 500 Index and is widely recognized for its principled environmental, social and governance practices. The company is an industry leader in value creation, supported by robust safety standards, superior execution and technical proficiency. Newmont was founded in 1921 and has been publicly traded since 1925.



Job description:

The Position

Newmont is seeking an Operational Intelligence Engineer to provide process control analytical solutions across our global mining operations. Utilising advanced process analysis techniques and technologies visualised through real-time monitoring systems, this strategic role bridges process control engineering, data analytics, and metallurgical expertise to transform our minerals processing data into actionable insights.

Role Overview - Operational Intelligence and Analytics

As our Operational Intelligence Engineer, you will leverage tools such as AVEVA OSIsoft PI (OSIPI), PI Asset Framework, PI Vision, Microsoft Power BI, Grafana, SEEQ, MS Excel, Python, MATLAB, R, Azure and emerging Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications, to provide descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive insights into our sites process control operational data. You will collaborate with central operational support, process control engineering, digital, metallurgical and site operational teams to develop analytical studies, continuous monitoring and/or closed loop process control solutions that have a direct impact on the stabilisation of mineral processing, maximisation of minerals recovery, and reduction of production costs.

Your expertise will help us make smarter decisions faster, maintain process stability, and continuously improve our production process controls. Taking on this role, you'll get the opportunity to shape the future of operational intelligence in the mining industry while working with cutting-edge technologies and a diverse range of teams across our global operations



Profile description:



Here is a snapshot of what your day to day activities will look like:

Design and implement process control analytics solutions that enhance the control systems ability to increase production, reduce cost, and improve overall plant efficiency
Create and maintain real-time monitoring systems for process control systems, critical processes and instrumentation
Create and maintain continuous real-time process control monitoring solutions focusing on Short Interval Control (SIC)
Create and maintain soft-sensor solutions that can be utilised for closed loop process control
Collaborate with centralised and site-based metallurgists, operational support engineers, digital SME's, process control engineers and plant operators to implement closed-loop control strategies focused towards stabilising and optimizing processing units like grinding circuits, flotation circuits, and leaching processes
Provide production insights via ad hoc analysis of historical process control data utilising descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analysis techniques and technologies
Establish and maintain KPI dashboards
Develop and maintain a centralised library of best practices for process analytics applications
Lead training sessions on process control analytics tools and mentor site-based technical resources
Participate in global mining process optimization initiatives and contribute to the community of practice

Your training skills and experience:

Minimum 3 years' experience in process control improvements, with significant operational experience in mineral processing or metallurgy
Industry qualifications in an associated field such as Data Science, Metallurgy, Process Control, IT.
Applied knowledge of AVEVA OSIsoft PI historian and PI Asset Framework systems
Data visualization using commercial-off-the-shelf products (i.e., AVEVA PI Vision, MS PowerBI
Grafana, SEEQ, MS Excel) or programmatic methods (i.e., Python, MATLAB, R)
Demonstrated success implementing analytical solutions within the mineral processing industry
Experience with crushing, grinding, flotation, leaching processes.
Strong understanding of mining-specific process variables, recovery calculations, and metallurgical accounting principles
Advanced computer literacy (word, excel, databases, statistical tools)
Skills in analytical story telling via building easy to read and to the point dashboarding
Applied knowledge of advanced process analytics techniques and technologies
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts to operational teams
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to identify and engage key stakeholders for collaboration
Customer-oriented, enjoys making connections and building trust with people both in-person and at remote locations
Motivated self-starter, goal-oriented, and strong problem-solving abilities
Active team player who celebrates and promotes team successes


We offer:



Your work environment

This permanent position will be offered on a flexible work arrangement based out of Newmont's Perth office in Subiaco, Western Australia. Applicants living in another Australian capital city location or residing near any Newmont Global Hubs will be considered if the ideal candidate.

The position will require flexibility to travel to Newmont office and/or mine site locations on a regular as required basis, including international travel.

Full and unrestricted eligibility for employment in Australia will be essential.

We understand no candidate will meet every single desired qualification. If your experience looks a little different from what we've identified and you think you can bring value to the role, we'd love to learn more about you!

If you feel that you could make a meaningful contribution in this role, we encourage you to discuss this opportunity with your current people leader and how it aligns with your longer-term career goals.

Job Title: Contract - Senior Consultant - Business Analyst (AI & Data)
Ad Text:
Our purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

About the Role

As technology evolves at an unprecedented pace, Datacom is looking for a Technical Business Analyst with a strong AI focus to bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI capabilities and real-world business outcomes. You will play a pivotal role in shaping AI-driven solutions, ensuring intelligent systems are designed, documented, and delivered with precision, clarity, and measurable business value.

What You'll DoRequirements Discovery & AI Solution Design
Understand the client's business processes, pain points, and opportunities where AI and automation can deliver value
Elicit and document requirements for AI/ML-powered solutions, including model inputs/outputs, training data needs, and business rules
Define and document AI use cases, including LLM integrations, predictive analytics, intelligent automation, and conversational AI workflows
Map data flows between AI models, consumers, and providers across integrated systems
Documentation & Artefact Creation
Create and manage JIRA tickets including:
Acceptance criteria
Implementation details
AI model behaviour expectations and edge cases
Consumer/provider mappings
Document all solution artefacts in Confluence, including:
UML sequence diagrams for AI-integrated workflows
Integration design patterns (AI APIs, model endpoints, orchestration layers)
Prompt engineering guidelines and LLM behaviour specifications
Mapping tables, endpoints, and environment configurations
Postman collections for AI API testing
Acceptance criteria for AI model outputs and responses
AI-Focused Facilitation & Collaboration
Facilitate workshops and discovery sessions focused on AI opportunity identification and solution scoping
Lead 3 Amigos sessions (Dev, Tester, and BA) with an emphasis on AI model validation, bias considerations, and responsible AI outcomes
Collaborate with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to translate business requirements into model specifications
Champion responsible AI principles, ensuring ethical considerations, explainability, and compliance are embedded into solution design
Data & Integration Design
Define data model requirements to support AI/ML training pipelines and inference outputs
Document HTTP operations, data types, and payload structures for AI model APIs
Design and maintain integration patterns connecting AI services to enterprise systems

Technical AbilityAPI & Integration Skills

Deep understanding of integration patterns: REST APIs, SOAP, event-driven architectures
API contract design and maintenance for AI model endpoints (e.g., OpenAI, Azure AI, AWS Bedrock, custom ML APIs)

Experience with Message Queuing technologies such as Kafka and ActiveMQ for AI pipeline event streaming
AI & Machine Learning Awareness
Working knowledge of LLMs (Large Language Models) — including prompt engineering, RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation), and fine-tuning concepts
Ability to document AI model behaviour requirements including confidence scoring, hallucination mitigation strategies, and output validation
Understanding of AI/ML pipeline stages: data ingestion, preprocessing, model training, deployment, and monitoring
Familiarity with AI platforms and services such as:
Azure OpenAI / Azure AI Studio
AWS SageMaker / Bedrock
Google Vertex AI
Hugging Face
Awareness of AI governance frameworks, including model explainability (XAI), fairness, and regulatory compliance (e.g., EU AI Act)
Estimation & Delivery
Provide reliable and traceable estimates for AI change requests and solution delivery, accounting for model iteration cycles and data preparation timelines
Design systems from a functional AI perspective, producing draft Design documents and leading client reviews through to sign-off
Contribute to AI solution roadmaps, identifying phased delivery approaches aligned to business priorities

Responsibilities

As a Technical Business Analyst (AI Focus), your day-to-day activities will include:

Produce drafts of Requirements and Design documents for AI-integrated solutions, lead client reviews, and achieve sign-off
Lead the Analysis and Design stage for AI initiatives, arranging and facilitating all relevant workshops and communication
Work closely with Technical Leads, Architects, and Data Scientists, ensuring they have sufficient understanding of functional requirements to design AI system architecture
Collaborate with Test Analysts to define AI-specific testing strategies, including model validation, A/B testing, and output quality benchmarks
Resolve ambiguities raised by developers and testers in relation to AI model specifications, communicating with clients as required
Monitor and communicate scope changes, AI-related risks, and project issues to Technical Leads and stakeholders proactively
Provide the team with AI industry best practices, emerging trends, and governance advice
Contribute to User Guides and AI usage documentation, supporting end-user adoption of AI-powered solutions
Ensure all design and compliance reviews are completed at required checkpoints, including responsible AI assessments

Tools & Experience
Project & Documentation - Confluence, JIRA
API Design & Testing - Swagger/OpenAPI, Postman, SOAP UI
AI Platforms - Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Hugging Face
Data & Monitoring - Splunk, Power BI
Development Environments - IntelliJ, Eclipse, VS CodeSource ControlGit, Bitbucket
Messaging & Streaming - Kafka, ActiveMQ
AI/ML Tooling - LangChain, Prompt Flow, Jupyter Notebooks (awareness)

Professional Qualities
5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst, with demonstrable exposure to AI, ML, or data-driven solutions
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to critically evaluate AI model outputs and business implications
Exceptional attention to detail, particularly when documenting complex AI workflows and edge cases
Deadline-driven with the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced AI delivery environment
Excellent communication skills — able to translate complex AI concepts for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborative team player with experience working in cross-functional Agile teams
A natural curiosity and passion for emerging AI technologies and their practical business applications

About Datacom

With over 6,200 people across our global offices and an annual revenue exceeding $1.2 billion, Datacom is one of Australasia's largest professional IT services companies. We have deep expertise in data centres, IT services, software engineering, application management, and AI-enabled solutions.

As AI reshapes industries, Datacom is at the forefront — partnering with clients to design and deliver intelligent, responsible, and impactful AI solutions. We are committed to hiring, developing, and promoting the best talent from diverse backgrounds.

We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

The role 
This company runs some of Australia's most recognised hotels. Their small, three-person engineering team is in the middle of a genuine transformation: moving from a legacy web and ops stack to one where AI agents do the heavy lifting. That means using AI to build and maintain 30+ websites with minimal human code, building agents that autonomously triage and complete workflows end to end, and integrating data across various platforms to personalise experiences at scale. The tech lead built the first AI-generated web pilot himself and it's nearly in production. You'd be the person who takes that further.
  
About you

You actively try to reduce how much you write by hand - AI agents and LLM workflows are your first tool, not a shortcut
You've shipped agentic or LLM-driven systems in production, using frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, Temporal or similar
Strong full stack or backend engineering foundation - you own integrations, APIs and data pipelines end to end
Comfortable in a small team where you'll touch web, data and AI work in the same week
Fluent in Python, TypeScript or JavaScript
You come from a role where your technical depth is visible, not hidden behind a large team

The offer

$160k + super
4 days in their Ultimo office, Fridays from home
Direct access to external AI and tech consultants working on the company's biggest strategic projects

If this role isn't quite right, but you're looking for other developer roles, feel free to get in touch anyway.

Our Story



4DMedical is a cutting-edge medical technology company specialising in advanced diagnostic imaging solutions that are revolutionising respiratory healthcare. We are an ASX200 company with several products that are cleared by the FDA and other international jurisdictions. 

 

Our innovative technology draws on measurement science and a deep understanding of physiological processes, and provides actionable insights into lung function, enabling early detection and precise treatment of respiratory conditions. Complementing our approach, we also develop sophisticated AI-driven lung imaging analysis of lung and cardiovascular structure to enhance the accuracy and depth of diagnostic information. Together, these technologies deliver a comprehensive suite of tools for personalized respiratory care. 






What can you expect as a Research Engineer at 4DMedical?

As a Research Engineer, you will join a tight-knit team investigating and evaluating new image processing techniques. You will be responsible for designing and evaluating, using scientific principles, the underlying methods used in our novel lung motion investigation software. The Research team leads the early stages of product development including concept generation and building and testing prototypes, as well as supporting 4DMedical’s formal software development, clinical studies, and imaging hardware R&D projects.

 

We seek to hire people with a variety of different skillsets. While your ability to undertake high quality research autonomously is important, we strongly value your ability to share your knowledge with the rest of the team, thereby ensuring an environment in which all team members grow and develop together.

 

Who are you?

You’ll embrace our goal of developing leading technologies to provide better health outcomes to patients. We are looking for creative researchers from a range of backgrounds, including engineering, mathematics, physics, computer science and data analytics, who can bring their unique skillsets to our lung imaging technologies.

We expect that you’ll have a degree in Engineering or Science, with experience undertaking world-class research in a field that can be related to image-based measurement - for example, traditional or deep-learning image processing techniques, Particle Image Velocimetry or related flow measurement methods, computer vision, and signal processing. You’ll be a highly capable programmer, with the ability to translate research ideas into prototypes to be tested scientifically.

 

If you have experience in 3D reconstruction techniques and X-ray imaging that’s great, and if not, we are happy to bring you up to speed. 

Experience in, or an understanding of, lung physiology or biomechanics would be highly advantageous but is not essential. 

 

People of all ages, genders, and backgrounds are encouraged to apply. We are an Equal Opportunity employer, providing a dynamic, supportive work environment.

 

Interstate applicants will be considered; however, you will need to be willing to relocate to Melbourne.

 
What we can offer you:
 

Hybrid working - 2 days from home
Easy access to Public Transport and very close to the University of Melbourne, Lygon St and the CBD
New, comfortable office space with end of trip facilities
Bonus annual leave scheme as well as company paid parental leave
Be a part of our wellness program including free fruit, coffee, table tennis, free gym and corporate class pass, and an Employee Assistance Program for you and your family
Rewards and Recognition Program including discounts for shopping and entertainment
Laptop and accessories provided for remote work
Regular social events




How to Apply



Please submit a current CV and a Cover Letter (Mandatory) which details your alignment with the requirements of the role. Applications that do not have a Cover Letter will not be considered. 



4DMedical provides an inclusive work environment and embraces the diverse talent of its people.

For more company information visit our website: www.4dmedical.com  



Should you have any queries regarding the position please contact the rec••••••••@4dmedical.com

Production Systems | Visual Intelligence | Real-World Signal Extraction

We build production-grade systems that extract reliable signal from messy, real-world visual data — systems trusted because they work consistently under real operating conditions, not because they look impressive in demos.

We are looking for a practical, production-minded Computer Vision Engineer who wants to build systems that create measurable operational impact.

This role carries real ownership, fast feedback loops, and the opportunity to work on systems deployed directly into live environments.


The Opportunity

You will work closely with domain experts, researchers, and engineers to build computer vision systems that:

extract actionable signal from video and image data

improve decision-making through proprietary data generation

create scalable, trusted operational intelligence

continuously improve through live feedback loops

turn unstructured visual information into measurable outcomes

You will own systems end-to-end, from problem framing, to ideation, execution and iteration.


What Makes This Role Different

We care about reliable systems, measurable outputs, and fast learning cycles.

You will operate with:

direct access to domain experts

high autonomy

fast deployment cycles

tight operational feedback loops

minimal bureaucracy

strong ownership expectations

This is an environment for builders who care more about useful systems than technical perfection.


What We're Looking For

You have already built systems that operate reliably in production environments.

You are comfortable working with:

imperfect or noisy visual data

ambiguous problem definitions

rapidly evolving requirements

fast iteration cycles

operationally critical systems

You move quickly, and are a practical thinker. You care about the outcomes and are likely stronger at shipping than presenting.


Technical Background

You will probably have strong experience across several of:

computer vision and image/video processing

Python and modern ML tooling

model training, evaluation, and deployment

production ML systems and infrastructure

monitoring, observability, and drift detection

dataset creation and labelling workflows

real-time or streaming data systems

experiment tracking and reproducibility

Experience working with production-grade data pipelines or operational AI systems is highly valuable.

We care more about judgement, execution speed, and production ownership than academic prestige.


What Success Looks Like

Within the first few months, we would expect:

production-ready CV systems deployed into live workflows

measurable improvements in signal quality and reliability

tighter iteration and feedback cycles

robust monitoring and retraining processes established

systems trusted by operators and stakeholders in production

Success in this role is measured by operational usefulness and system reliability.


Compensation

Compensation is flexible and designed to attract exceptional individuals.

Permanent, full-time  

Build the future of AI inside local government

LET’S EXPLORE THE JOB 

Artificial Intelligence is transforming how organisations operate, and Lake Macquarie City Council is embracing that opportunity.

We are seeking an Artificial Intelligence Engineer to design and deliver practical, enterprise-grade AI solutions that enhance operational efficiency, staff experience, and digital service delivery across Council.

Working within our Data and Analytics Team, you will develop and implement intelligent solutions including AI-powered assistants, automation tools, and machine learning capabilities using technologies such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, Azure AI, and Power Platform AI Builder.

This is a rare opportunity to work at the intersection of emerging AI technology and real-world impact. Your solutions will help automate processes, enhance decision making, and improve how staff and the community interact with our services.

You’ll collaborate across business areas and with technology partners to build secure, scalable and responsible AI solutions, while also helping uplift AI capability across our organisation through pilots, demonstrations and innovation initiatives.

If you’re excited about building practical AI solutions that make a meaningful difference, this is the role for you. 

What you’ll be doing:

Designing and developing AI-enabled tools, assistants and automation solutions

Building and maintaining AI agents, chatbots and intelligent digital assistants

Implementing machine learning and predictive analytics where appropriate

Read the position statement click here and learn more about this exciting opportunity.

You enjoy collaborating with stakeholders to understand challenges, translating them into practical AI-enabled solutions that streamline processes and improve experiences for staff and customers.

You are also mindful of the responsible use of AI, ensuring solutions are secure, ethical and aligned with governance frameworks.

Whether you have worked in enterprise, consulting, or emerging tech environments, what matters most is your ability to build practical AI solutions and continuously explore what’s possible.

You bring:

Degree qualification in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science or related field (or equivalent experience)

Experience working with Microsoft technologies including Microsoft 365, Power Platform or Copilot environments

 Programming or scripting capability (Python, JavaScript, C# or similar)

 Experience integrating systems using APIs and modern architectures

Experience working with ChatGPT Enterprise, Copilot Studio, Power Automate, or workflow automation tools will be highly regarded.

Hi there! We’re Five Good Friends. 

We’re an approved aged care and NDIS service provider helping our Members (others call them clients) live vibrantly in their own homes. We wrap a care team around them and their families, supported by innovative software that coordinates in the background—so our teams can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care. 

We’re proud to be a place where purpose meets impact. Every day, our work contributes to helping people live their best lives, and that starts with our people. 

As more people choose Five Good Friends, we’re building out our Platform team to strengthen the foundations that power our technology and services. That’s where you come in. 

About the Role 

We’re looking for an AI System Analyst to help us understand, define, and shape how our platform and operations work end-to-end. 

This role sits at the intersection of business, systems, and engineering. You’ll work across processes, SaaS platforms, integrations, and data turning real-world operational complexity into clear, structured knowledge that supports both delivery teams and AI-assisted ways of working. 

You won’t be working in isolation or producing static documentation. Instead, you’ll be embedded in the work, partnering closely with engineers, product, and operational teams to uncover how things work, identify gaps, and bring clarity to ambiguity. 

You'll be part of a hands-on, genuinely collaborative team of engineers, system thinkers and innovators. Think getting around a whiteboard together, unpacking problems, challenging ideas, and designing solutions as a group. The work is substantive; the problems are challenging; and expectations on the whole Platform team to continue delivering are high. 

This is a hybrid role, balancing in-office collaboration with focused work from home. When you’re in, you’ll be based in our modern South Bank office, close to great cafes and restaurants, with easy access to public transport. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Investigating and documenting business processes, workflows, and system behaviours 
Tracing how workflows across SaaS platforms, integrations, APIs, and automations 
Defining business rules, operational terminology, and data definitions 
Creating structured, usable knowledge that supports engineering delivery and AI-enabled workflows 
Identifying gaps, inconsistencies, and dependencies across systems and processes 
Collaborating with engineers, product, and stakeholders to ensure shared understanding before delivery 
Supporting more consistent, scalable ways of working across the platform 

About You 

You’re naturally curious and think in systems, you want to understand how things actually work, not just how they’re supposed to. You’re comfortable navigating complexity, asking the right questions, and bringing structure to ambiguity. 

You enjoy working closely with both technical and non-technical people, and you’re confident moving between operational conversations and technical detail. 

You’ll bring: 

Strong systems thinking and analytical capability 
Experience working across complex operational or SaaS-heavy environments 
The ability to translate real-world processes into clear, structured documentation 
Strong written and verbal communication skills 
Experience working in agile or iterative delivery environments 
A proactive, detail-oriented approach with a focus on quality and clarity 

You’re also energised by AI and actively using AI-assisted tools or working with AI/ML concepts, and excited by how data platforms enable intelligent systems. 

Bonus points for experience with APIs, integrations, automation tools, data or process mapping, or working alongside engineering teams. 

What’s in It for You 

You’ll help shape how our platform and the knowledge behind it supports better care and stronger outcomes for our members. Your work will enable faster, clearer, and more confident delivery across the organisation, while contributing to how we embed AI into the way we work. 

And a few more things we think you’ll value: 

Competitive salary package that recognises your skills and experience 
Flexible hybrid work arrangements to support focus and collaboration 
Annual $500 health and wellbeing allowance 
Additional annual leave accrual after your second year and the option to purchase extra leave 
Free confidential counselling through Sonder 
Parental leave top-ups for eligible caregivers, including superannuation support 
Extend our care to a loved one at a special rate 
A workplace with an employee engagement score 17% above industry average 
And after 5 years of continuous service—a trip to Okinawa, Japan, the community that inspired Five Good Friends 

How to Apply 

If this role sounds like you, click the apply button and follow the prompts. Please include your CV and a cover letter telling us a bit about yourself and why this role is a great fit. 

Please, no external recruiters or agencies.

12 + 12 months, $$$ negotiable

Software Engineer — Full Stack & Cloud - Sydney Based

This is a rare opportunity to join a small, high-trust engineering squad and grow into a genuinely full-stack, cloud-native AI native software engineer - with real mentorship, real ownership, and a team that's invested in your development from day one. 

This is not a Grad role but, we're also not looking for someone who's done it all. We're looking for someone who's hungry to learn, takes pride in their craft, and wants to build something meaningful in a regulated financial services environment where engineering quality actually matters.

If you have strong fundamentals, the curiosity to keep growing, and the self-awareness to ask the right questions - we want to talk to you.

What you'll be doing

→ Contributing to scalable AWS-native infrastructure using CDK alongside experienced engineers

→ Building backend services and APIs in Java 21 / Spring Boot with guidance and code review

→ Developing TypeScript frontend features and end-to-end user experiences

→ Working with PostgreSQL, Aurora and RDS as you develop your data skills

→ Learning to use AI coding assistants effectively as a daily productivity tool

→ Growing into operational ownership; monitoring, observability, and continuous improvement

→ Participating in planning, demos and stakeholder engagement as your confidence builds

The stack you'll grow into

Hi there! We’re Five Good Friends. 

We’re an approved aged care and NDIS service provider helping our Members (others call them clients) live vibrantly in their own homes. We wrap a care team around them and their families, supported by innovative software that coordinates in the background—so our teams can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care. 

We’re proud to be a place where purpose meets impact. Every day, our work contributes to helping people live their best lives, and that starts with our people. As more people choose Five Good Friends, we’re building out our Platform team to strengthen the foundations that power our technology and services. That’s where you come in. 

About the Role 

We’re looking for an AI-first Full Stack Software Engineer to build the systems that support how we deliver care at scale. 

You’ll work across frontend and backend, creating reliable, maintainable solutions that connect real-world care experiences with the technology behind them. You’ll contribute across everything from user-facing features to platform capabilities. 

You'll be part of a hands-on, genuinely collaborative team of engineers, system thinkers and innovators. Think getting around a whiteboard together, unpacking problems, challenging ideas, and designing solutions as a group. The work is substantive; the problems are challenging; and expectations on the whole Platform team to continue delivering are high. 

This is a hybrid role, balancing in-office collaboration with focused work from home. When you’re in, you’ll be based in our modern South Bank office, close to great cafes and restaurants, with easy access to public transport. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Designing and building scalable, high-quality full stack applications — with AI-assisted tooling (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code) as a natural part of your development workflow 
Developing intuitive, responsive front-end experiences  
Building robust backend services, APIs, and integrations — including services that connect to and support agentic and AI-driven workflows 
Collaborating closely with engineers, product, and data teams to deliver end-to-end solutions  
Contributing to system design, architecture, and technical decision-making  
Writing clean, well-tested and secure code with AI copilot development tools as a genuine part of how you build — and continuously raising the bar on engineering quality 

About You 

You enjoy building end-to-end solutions and care about creating systems that are both functional and well designed. You’re collaborative, take ownership, and bring clarity to complex problems. AI is already part of how you work — not something you’re curious about eventually, but something you reach for every day. 

You’ll bring: 

Hands-on experience with Node.js and TypeScript on the backend, and React (Vite) on the frontend 
Strong fundamentals in software design and problem-solving 
Experience building APIs and working with distributed systems, deployed into Azure and AWS 
Comfortable with Docker for local development and containerised workflows 
A testing mindset — writing unit tests with Jest and end-to-end tests with Playwright 
Familiar with GitHub for source control, branching strategies, and collaborative PR reviews 
Confidence working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders   

You’re also energised by AI—actively using AI-assisted tools or working with AI/ML concepts, and excited by how data platforms enable intelligent systems. 

Bonus points for experience with modern JavaScript frameworks, cloud platforms, or product-led environments. 

What’s in It for You 

You’ll help build something from the ground up, shaping how our technology supports better care and stronger connections for our members. You’ll be part of a team that values simplicity, ownership, and real impact, with the opportunity to do meaningful work and see the difference it makes. 

And a few more things we think you’ll value: 

Competitive salary package that recognises your skills and experience 
Flexible hybrid work arrangements to support focus and collaboration 
Annual $500 health and wellbeing allowance 
Additional annual leave accrual after your second year and the option to purchase extra leave 
Free confidential counselling through Sonder 
Parental leave top-ups for eligible caregivers, including superannuation support 
Extend our care to a loved one at a special rate 
A workplace with an employee engagement score 17% above industry average 
And after 5 years of continuous service—a trip to Okinawa, Japan, the community that inspired Five Good Friends 

How to Apply 

If this role sounds like you, click the apply button and follow the prompts. Please include your CV and a cover letter telling us a bit about yourself and why this role is a great fit. 

Please, no external recruiters or agencies.

The Data Governance Specialist plays a critical role within the Enterprise Data & Analytics section, reporting to the Chief Data & Analytics Officer. The role is responsible for supporting the development, implementation, and ongoing management of our data and AI governance framework.

This role sits at the intersection of data strategy, operations, technical data platform development, and compliance, working across disciplines to ensure our data assets are accurate, secure, and used responsibly across the agency, and enabling ACIC to understand, manage and reduce risks relevant to its data holdings. This includes governance of advanced analytics and AI-enabled capabilities deployed in ACIC-specific functions and corporate contexts.

Role responsibilities

As a Data Governance Specialist, you will:

Enterprise Data and AI Governance Frameworks

Co-lead the end-to-end development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the Enterprise Data Governance Framework, including creation of tailored policies, standards and operational procedures.
Drive the implementation and adoption of the Enterprise Data Strategy, ensuring strategic objectives are translated into practical, enterprise-wide business processes.
Lead the creation of high-quality strategic guidance, position papers, and best practice frameworks that provide clear, authoritative advice to stakeholders across the agency.
Interpret and apply whole-of-government data and AI policies in an intelligence agency context, providing expert guidance on navigating nuanced exemptions and alternate obligations to ensure practical alignment.

Data and AI Assurance, Risk Assessment and Control Design

Provide expert data governance assurance support, ensuring all AI and data management practices comply with relevant legal, regulatory, and whole-of-government and agency-specific policies.
Perform complex AI risk assessments for existing and proposed AI and advanced analytics use cases, designing and recommending/applying governance controls to ensure systems are lawful, ethical, secure, explainable and subject to appropriate human oversight.
Provide authoritative advice on data and AI related risks, controls, oversight mechanisms and reporting to support informed decision making by senior governance bodies.

Governance by Design Across Data and Analytics Platforms

Collaborate with data engineering, analytics and platform teams to embed data and AI governance by design, ensuring data and AI controls are integrated across the full lifecycle from design to decommissioning.
Act as a primary conduit between technical specialists and business areas, translating complex technical data architectures and governance requirements into clear, actionable business insights and advice.

Stewardship and Enterprise Governance Forums

Operationalise the Data Stewardship model by onboarding, training, and providing ongoing mentorship to Data Stewards, Franchise Data Governance Teams, and other key stakeholders to ensure roles are active and effective.
Lead the secretariat function for the Enterprise Data Committee and related governance forums, supporting effective governance decision making through high quality advice, papers, reporting and coordination.

Capability Uplift, Literacy and Change Enablement

Navigate and resolve complex data governance challenges where there is no clear precedent, applying high-level problem-solving skills to balance competing priorities.
Lead the design, delivery and communication of education and training to uplift data and AI literacy, tailoring materials for both technical and non technical audiences.
Support enterprise adoption of data and AI governance frameworks through change enablement and focused stakeholder engagement, identifying emerging data needs and barriers to effective data use.

YOUR SKILLS AND CAPABILITES

We invite applications from people with experience, skills and attributes aligned to the following areas:

Enterprise Data and AI Governance Frameworks

Demonstrated experience developing, implementing and operationalising enterprise data and AI governance frameworks, including policies, standards and procedures, within complex regulatory environments.
Ability to tailor data governance and data management policies, standards, frameworks, procedures and practices according to legal, regulatory and operational contexts.

Data and AI Assurance, Risk Assessment and Control Design

Knowledge of data and AI ethics, with experience ensuring that data and AI use cases align with ethical standards and guardrails.
Experience applying AI governance principles, including performing complex risk assessments, designing and applying appropriate controls, and managing escalation processes.
Proven ability to govern data and AI in regulated, sensitive or high risk environments, such as intelligence, law enforcement, national security or similarly complex regulated private sector contexts.
Comprehensive knowledge of modern, best practice data governance and data management policies, standards, frameworks, procedures, and practices.
Advanced understanding of how emerging technologies (particularly AI) translate into new risks and opportunities, and the ability to adapt data and AI governance approaches to meet rapidly evolving requirements.

Governance by Design Across Data and Analytics Platforms

Familiarity with modern data architectures, including medallion, and domain-driven data architectures, and the ability to collaborate with data engineers and data scientists to embed data governance controls directly into data platforms.
Ability to work collaboratively with data scientists, engineers, and legal teams to ensure governance by design is a collaborative outcome informed by the technical realities.
Experience in establishing metadata standards and managing data catalogues to improve data discoverability and usability and trust.

Stewardship and Enterprise Governance Forums

Experience leading secretariat functions for high-level committees or working groups.
Demonstrated ability to engage senior stakeholders on data and AI risk appetite, balancing innovation, operational value and harm minimisation.

Capability Uplift, Literacy and Change Enablement

Sophisticated understanding of how data provides business value and demonstrated experience in providing business value through data initiatives.  
Experience driving organisational change to support adoption of new data and AI governance processes across diverse business areas.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a proven ability to create a compelling story around the business value of data-related initiatives.
Ability to adapt communication styles to bridge the gap between technical specialists and non-technical audiences to build shared understanding of governance risks and responsibilities.
Ability to design and deliver data and AI literacy programs and training materials tailored to technical and non-technical audiences.

Personal Attributes

A strong commitment to continual learning and professional development, particularly in relation to evolving data, analytics and AI technologies and the environments in which they operate.
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with ambiguity and complexity, making sound, evidence-based decisions when faced with conflicting technical requirements or shifting policy landscapes.

Eligibility

Citizenship – To be eligible for employment with the ACIC, applicants must be an Australian citizen.
Security Clearance - The Australian community expects ACIC employees to maintain the highest levels of security and integrity. Successful applicants will be required to obtain and maintain a minimum Negative Vetting 1 security clearance.
Pre-Employment Screening – All candidates must satisfy an Organisational Suitability Assessment (OSA) prior to being offered employment at the ACIC.

The ACIC is committed to diversity and inclusion. We celebrate and champion diversity to ensure we reflect the community we serve, and pride ourselves on our vibrant culture. We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, women, people with disability, people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those who identify as LGBTIQ+, neurodiverse, mature aged employees and carers.

More information about our conditions is available in the ACIC Enterprise Agreement 2024-2027 on our website.

Context

We are seeking 2 Cloud SMEs with hands on design and build experience to support our Federal Government Client.

Our Client's Directorate ("CsD" here on) designs, assures, engineers, delivers, and sustains the Department's hybrid and multi‑cloud platforms that underpin the overall Departmental Information Environment.

CsD provides enterprise cloud landing zones, shared platform services, engineering standards, and repeatable solution patterns across multiple hyper-scale and sovereign cloud providers. These platforms enable secure application hosting, migration, modernisation, and ongoing digital transformation across the Department's portfolio.

To support continued platform delivery, uplift, and assurance, CsD is engaging 2 specialist Cloud Engineering SMEs to provide senior, hands‑on engineering leadership across hybrid and multi‑cloud environments.

Location

Location will either be Canberra or Melbourne, strictly.

Expectation is the engineering will be onsite 5 days a week, strictly.

Please do not apply if you can-not work on site in Melbourne or Canberra.

Role Overview

The Technical Engineering Specialist is a deeply hands‑on cloud engineer and platform builder, embedded within the CsD delivery teams.

The role is responsible for the design, build, assurance, support and continuous improvement of enterprise‑grade cloud platforms and enabling services.

This position spans:

Cloud platform and landing zone engineering

Hybrid and multi‑cloud integration

Secure workload migration and modernisation

Automation, DevSecOps, and operational assurance

The role requires practical hands-on delivery experience, strong engineering judgement, and the ability to operate as a senior subject‑matter expert within highly regulated environments.

Key Responsibilities

Technical Leadership & Delivery Collaboration

Act as a senior hands‑on technical leader, mentoring cloud engineers and uplift delivery capability.

Collaborate closely with security, application, data, and network teams to deliver integrated solutions.

Define, document, and enforce engineering standards, patterns, and reference architectures.

Provide authoritative technical advice into design reviews, assurance activities, and senior technical forums.

Cloud Platform Engineering (Hands‑On)

Design, build, and operate enterprise cloud platforms and landing zones supporting IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS workloads.

Engineer hybrid and multi‑cloud architectures, integrating public cloud services with private cloud and on‑premises environments.

Act as a primary hands‑on engineer in one to two cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP), with working delivery experience across additional platforms.

Contribute directly to platform uplift activities, technical remediation, and production issue resolution.

Migration & Modernisation Delivery

Execute cloud migration programs, including:

Application discovery and technical assessment

Landing zone readiness and assurance

Workload migration, cutover, and optimisation

Support modernisation approaches such as re‑hosting, re‑platforming, refactoring, and cloud‑native transformation.

Develop and apply repeatable engineering patterns enabling migration and modernisation at scale across multiple portfolios.

Networking & Hybrid Connectivity

Design and implement secure cloud networking, including:

VPC/VNet architectures, subnetting, routing, and multi‑account/subscription models

Hub‑and‑spoke and transit networking patterns

DNS services (Azure DNS, Route 53, Cloud DNS)

Hybrid connectivity (ExpressRoute, Direct Connect, VPN)

Engineer and assure integration with Departmental on-premise and enterprise network environments.

Troubleshoot and remediate complex hybrid networking issues in live environments.

DevSecOps & Automation

Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for platforms and services using Terraform, Bicep, ARM, or CloudFormation.

Engineer CI/CD and DevSecOps pipelines that embed:

Security controls

Compliance validation

Quality and policy enforcement

Drive automation, standardisation, and environment consistency across all cloud platforms.

Eliminate manual processes through programmatic and pipeline‑driven delivery.

Containers, Kubernetes & Serverless Platforms

Engineer platforms supporting containerised and microservices workloads, including EKS, AKS, GKE, or equivalent services.

Support serverless architectures using services such as AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, API Gateway, and Cloud Run.

Implement platform‑level capabilities for:

Observability and monitoring

Scaling and resilience

Secure runtime operations

Security‑by‑Design & Compliance (Practitioner‑Led)

Apply a security‑first, build‑time mindset across all engineering activities.

Engineer and operate IAM solutions, including roles, policies, least‑privilege models, and identity federation.

Implement and maintain:

Network security controls

Encryption at rest and in transit

Centralised logging, monitoring, and alerting

Ensure platform and workload compliance with ISM, PSPF, and Essential Eight requirements.

Infrastructure as Code (IaC) – Dedicated Focus

Own and evolve IaC foundations for landing zones and shared services.

Implement version control, modular design, and re‑usable patterns for enterprise consumption.

Enforce policy‑as‑code and guardrails aligned to the Departmental security and architecture standards.

Use IaC as the primary mechanism for environment provisioning, change control, and auditability.

FinOps & Cost Engineering

Engineer platforms with cost visibility, allocation, and accountability.

Implement tagging, cost allocation, and usage reporting across subscriptions/accounts.

Support chargeback/ show-back models and optimisation initiatives.

Partner with governance and platform teams to:

Reduce waste

Optimise consumption

Balance cost, performance, and security outcomes

Required Experience & Capabilities

Proven hands‑on delivery of enterprise‑scale cloud platforms and landing zones in large, complex environments.

Demonstrated execution of cloud migration and modernisation programs across public or private sector organisations.

Strong practical expertise in:

Hybrid and multi‑cloud engineering

Cloud networking and IAM

DevSecOps and automation

Kubernetes and/or serverless platforms

Ability to operate as a senior SME, combining technical authority with direct engineering delivery.

Strong stakeholder communication skills within highly regulated environments.

 Relevant Qualifications & Industry‑Credible Certifications

Formal Qualifications (Desirable)

Bachelor’s degree in ICT, Computer Science, or related discipline, or equivalent industry experience.

Cloud & Platform Certifications (Highly Desirable)

 Security Requirements

Must hold, or be eligible to obtain and maintain, Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1) clearance. NV2 (Desirable)

Must comply with all Fed. Gov and Departmental security policies, procedures, and legislative requirements for the duration of the engagement.

Hi there! We’re Five Good Friends. 

We’re an approved aged care and NDIS service provider helping our Members (others call them clients) live vibrantly in their own homes. We wrap a care team around them and their families, supported by innovative software that coordinates in the background—so our teams can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care. 

We’re proud to be a place where purpose meets impact. Every day, our work contributes to helping people live their best lives, and that starts with our people. 

As more people choose Five Good Friends, we’re building out our Platform team to strengthen the foundations that power our technology and services. That’s where you come in. 

About the Role 

This is not a standard data analytics or engineering role. 

It's about thinking in systems and building our data platform from first principles. As an AI Data Engineer in our Platform team, you will design and build the end-to-end data foundations that translate our real-world processes — executed by both human teams and agentic workflows — into clean, structured, and scalable data models our business can rely on. 

AI is not a side capability in this role. AI is a core part of how the Platform team works and how you will be expected to design, build & manage your data products. You'll use AI tooling throughout the full data engineering lifecycle — from scoping and schema design through to design reviews, refactoring, and governance – and you'll build medallion data layers that serve AI consumers with the same rigour and intent you bring to human consumers. 

You'll be part of a hands-on, genuinely collaborative team of engineers, system thinkers and innovators. Think getting around a whiteboard together, unpacking problems, challenging ideas, and designing solutions as a group. The work is substantive; the problems are challenging; and expectations on the whole Platform team to continue delivering are high. 

This is a hybrid role, balancing focused work from home with in-office collaboration. When you're in, you'll be based in our modern South Bank office, with easy access to great cafes, restaurants, and public transport. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Data Modelling & Schema Design — Designing, implementing and maintaining clear, structured data models based on both existing models and first-principles thinking, using AI-assisted tooling throughout — from scoping and schema design to reviewing existing models, reasoning through edge cases, and refactoring legacy structures safely. 
Medallion Architecture – Designing, implementing and maintaining bronze, silver, and gold data infrastructure layers that deliver accurate, reliable perspectives to both AI and human consumers — ensuring each layer is purpose-built, clearly governed, and trusted by the teams and systems that depend on it. 
Data Ingestion & ELT — Designing, implementing and maintaining ELT pipelines that extract data from source systems, aligned with how we generate & consume data. 
Data Quality & Governance — Working closely with our colleagues to ensure our implemented data schemas accurately reflect Five Good Friends' business process taxonomy, organisational & industry required rules ontology.  
Software Engineering Collaboration — Working side-by-side with a core team of talented software engineers, systems analysts, and operational stakeholders to deliver comprehensive Platform solutions that span distributed deployment topologies. 
AI-Enabled Ways of Working — Actively embracing AI productivity tooling (such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, VS Code AI extensions) across schema design, project scoping, documentation, and refactoring — and help establish responsible, effective patterns for AI-assisted data engineering across the team. 

About You 

You think in systems and care about how things connect, not just how they work individually. You’re collaborative, hold a high bar for quality, and confidently challenge ideas to bring clarity to ambiguity. 

You’ll bring: 

Proven SQL and data modelling capabilities
Experience with a variety of data pipeline, integration and ELT tools and approaches 
A detail-oriented mindset with a focus on data integrity and consistency 
Confidence communicating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders 
A proactive, ownership-driven approach to your work 
Willingness to collaborate and co-design comprehensive & integrated solutions that span beyond the data platform. 

You’re also energised by AI and actively using AI-assisted tools or working with AI/ML concepts, and excited by how data platforms enable the design, delivery and management of intelligent platform-based solutions. 

Bonus points for experience with Snowflake, Azure Fabric, PostgreSQL, Crunchy, dbt, Airflow/Dagster in particular. 

What’s in It for You 

You’ll help build something from the ground up—shaping how data is designed, used, and trusted across the organisation. Your work will directly support better outcomes for our members in an environment that values simplicity, ownership, and real impact. It’s a role where you can do your best work and see the difference it makes. 

 And a few more things we think you’ll value: 

Competitive salary package that recognises your skills and experience 
Flexible hybrid work arrangements to support focus and collaboration 
Annual $500 health and wellbeing allowance 
Additional annual leave accrual after your second year and the option to purchase extra leave 
Free confidential counselling through Sonder 
Parental leave top-ups for eligible caregivers, including superannuation support 
Extend our care to a loved one at a special rate 
A workplace with an employee engagement score 17% above industry average 
And after 5 years of continuous service—a trip to Okinawa, Japan, the community that inspired Five Good Friends 

How to Apply 

If this role sounds like you, click the apply button and follow the prompts. Please include your CV and a cover letter telling us a bit about yourself and why this role is a great fit. 

Please, no external recruiters or agencies.

Solutions Engineer

What Kestral is all about:

Kestral is passionate about creating the industry standard in Radiology and Pathology information technology. We provide cutting-edge software solutions that make a real difference in healthcare delivery across Australia.

At Kestral, we thrive on working with talented, passionate people who embrace learning opportunities, seek continuous improvement, and get a kick out of finding better ways to do things. We're a tight-knit team dedicated to excellence, with a shared vision of setting the benchmark in healthcare IT.

Join us and become part of an exceptional team who are genuinely excited about what we do and what we're achieving.

 

What you'll be doing at Kestral:

We are seeking a Solution Engineer to join our team. This role is responsible for the delivery, implementation, handover, and ongoing maintenance of technical solutions across project and operational environments. The role combines hands-on technical execution with strong project delivery focus, ensuring solutions are designed, deployed, supported, and maintained in line with business and operational requirements.

Working closely with project, development, and support teams, the Solution Engineer contributes to solution design, troubleshooting, system integration, upgrades, and continuous improvement. The role plays a key part in ensuring reliable service delivery, smooth transition from project to operations, and effective ongoing support of deployed solutions.




Key Responsibilities

Project & Service Delivery

Deliver technical components of assigned projects from initiation through to completion

Analyse, design, and implement solution components to meet business/technical requirements

Support system deployments, upgrades, and implementation activities

Deliver solutions in accordance with agreed scope, timelines, and quality standards

Identify and manage delivery risks, issues, and changes

Solution Support & Maintenance

Provide high-level technical and functional support for deployed solutions

Support ongoing maintenance, administration, and enhancement of systems and integrations

Resolve technical issues in delivery and production environments

Support post-implementation upgrades, patches, and improvements

Technical Analysis & Integration

Analyse system behaviour and data flows

Support integrations with third-party systems

Assist with testing, validation, and performance analysis

Planning & Documentation

Support project and maintenance planning

Produce and maintain solution documentation and runbooks

Ensure effective handover to operations

Quality, Risk & Compliance

Ensure compliance with organisational standards

Participate in testing and QA activities

Promote secure and maintainable solutions

Stakeholder Engagement

Engage with internal stakeholders and clients

Communicate progress, issues, and outcomes clearly







What We’re Looking For

 

Essential Qualifications & Experience

Relevant tertiary qualification in IT, engineering, or related discipline

2+ years’ experience in solution engineering or technical delivery

Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills

Experience supporting deployed systems




Desirable Experience

Exposure to operational support environments

Experience with integrations and system lifecycle management




Skills & Characteristics:

Strong ability to collaborate and work well in a team.

Professional approach to work and work output.

Passion for AI and commitment to continuous improvement




The perks: what we can offer you:

Joining Kestral means becoming part of a friendly, high-performance team in an engaging work environment.

Our benefits include:

Employee Share Purchase Plan from our parent company Constellation Software Inc.

Flexible leave offerings, including opportunities to purchase additional leave

Support for flexible working arrangements

Comprehensive EAP Support for your mental and physical wellbeing

Continuous learning opportunities with on-the-job training and external development options




Want to get started?

If this role and our team sound like the perfect match for you, we'd love to hear from you! Please submit your CV and optional cover letter detailing your experience and why you're interested in joining the Kestral team.

Kestral is a subsidiary of Jonas Software, the leading provider of enterprise management software for more than 40,000 customers across 15 countries. Jonas Software is a 100% owned subsidiary of Constellation Software Inc., headquartered in Toronto and traded on the TSX (CSU.TO).

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - AI Enablement (AU remote)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. 

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney, with a second campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth, & Adelaide. You have flexibility in how and where you work — whether that's from one of our spaces, from home, or a mix of both. This role is remote-friendly within Australia, so you can choose the setup that empowers you and your team to do your best work.

Job Description


About the Group/Team

Canva's AI Platform Group serves as the foundation for AI innovation across the company, and achieving our GenAI ambitions depends on its ability to scale effectively. Within this group, the AI Enablement teams provide critical solution expertise to researchers and AI builders globally, helping them navigate platform capabilities, apply expert domain knowledge, optimise implementations, and accelerate the journey from experimentation to production. These teams bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and reliable, production-ready features, ensuring that innovation translates into real user value.

About the Role/Specialty

Please note, this is not a model building role

This role sits at the intersection of solution expertise and platform engineering, requiring deep technical expertise across ML domains combined with strong platform knowledge. The senior MLE will context-switch between facilitating model evaluations, data pipeline, optimising inference performance and working directly with product teams building solutions.

The nature of enablement work demands someone who operates as a force multiplier, finding satisfaction not just in solving technical problems but in enabling others to move faster. The role operates in a high-velocity environment where priorities shift rapidly based on team needs, requiring both technical excellence and pragmatic judgment. This combination of deep expertise and adaptive problem-solving is critical to accelerating the delivery of GenAI capabilities across Canva, removing friction from the path to production and ensuring product teams across the company can ship AI-powered features at the pace and quality our ambitions require.

What you’ll do (responsibilities):


Responsibilities will evolve as Canva's needs change, but here's what the role will focus on initially:

Act as a solution expert across ML domains including evaluations, training, inference, data pipelines, labelling, and optimisation

Work directly alongside product teams as a trusted partner, helping them navigate technical challenges and arrive at effective solutions

Provide expert guidance on platform capabilities, helping teams understand what's possible and how to get there efficiently

Develop blueprints, patterns, and paved roads that allow other teams to follow proven approaches and accelerate their own implementations

Debug and resolve complex issues, identifying root causes and sharing learnings broadly

Balance hands-on problem solving with strategic thinking about how to scale enablement impact across the organisation

You're probably a match if you have any of the following:

Strong Python expertise with experience in ML frameworks

Experience working with Kubernetes

Experience or knowledge of building data pipelines

Experience or knowledge of model evaluations

Experience with inference optimisation and serving infrastructure

Experience supporting or working closely with research, data science, or product teams

Excellent debugging and problem-solving skills across the full ML stack

Ability to context-switch effectively across multiple concurrent projects

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to varied audiences

A collaborative mindset with a focus on enabling others to succeed

Additional Information


Don't tick all the boxes? Don't worry about that - nobody does!  

We’d still love to hear from you! At Canva, we know that great engineers come from a variety of backgrounds, and we value passion, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as specific experience. If you're excited about this role but don’t tick every box, we encourage you to apply, you might a great fit in ways you didn’t expect!

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out  lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

All interviews are conducted virtually
